figures.gloss: In the oldest Hebrew poetry, the storm-God who crushes the heads of Leviathan and Rahab — before becoming the sole God beyond all pantheons.
figures.facet: The shard that swallowed the others: a storm-and-dragon-slayer who became radical monotheism.
figures.provenance: Frank Moore Cross, Canaanite Myth and Hebrew Epic (1973); Mark S. Smith, The Early History of God: Yahweh and the Other Deities in Ancient Israel (2002).

figures.gloss: In Christian belief, crucified and risen on the third day — death undone for all who follow.
figures.facet: The one case its tradition insists is literal history, not seasonal symbol — which is exactly the fault-line of the whole comparison.
figures.provenance: The canonical Gospels (Mark, Matthew, Luke, John); Larry Hurtado, Lord Jesus Christ: Devotion to Jesus in Earliest Christianity (2003).

date.note: The historical Jesus ~4 BCE–30 CE; the risen Christ as worshipped develops after.

figures.gloss: The tree of life at the center of the garden, and four rivers running out to the four quarters of the earth.
figures.provenance: Genesis 2:8–14 (Masoretic Text); E. A. Speiser, Genesis (Anchor Bible, 1964), on the geography and sourcing of the four named rivers.

figures.gloss: In Kabbalah, the infinite-without-end, beyond name or attribute, from which the ten sefirot pour out to make a knowable God.
figures.provenance: Sefer ha-Zohar (attributed to Moses de León, late 13th c.); Gershom Scholem, Major Trends in Jewish Mysticism (1941).

date.note: The Kabbalistic Ein Sof crystallizes ~12th–13th c. (Sefer ha-Zohar).

figures.gloss: The patriarch of Genesis 12–25 — called out of Ur/Haran to a promised land, father of Isaac by Sarah and Ishmael by Hagar, and recipient of God's covenant sealed in circumcision. Judaism, Christianity, and Islam each route their claim on the one God through his line, and Bahá'í teaching likewise reveres him as a forerunner of progressive revelation.
figures.facet: The keystone that gives the whole composite tradition its name yet appeared in no dedicated shard — three faiths read the same covenant story to opposite ends of one family tree, Isaac's line carrying Judaism and Christianity, Ishmael's carrying Islam.
figures.provenance: Genesis 12–25 (Masoretic Text); Qur'an 2:124–141, 37:99–113 (Ibrahim); Jon D. Levenson, Inheriting Abraham: The Legacy of the Patriarch in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam (2012).

date.note: Traditional Genesis chronology sets the patriarchal narratives in the early-to-mid second millennium BCE; no extrabiblical record of Abraham exists, so this is a narrative setting, not an attested historical date.

figures.gloss: The Hebrew leader raised in Pharaoh's household who brings the Israelites out of Egyptian bondage, receives the Law at Sinai, and dies within sight of the promised land he is barred from entering. Counted a prophet in Judaism, a type of Christ in Christian reading, and named more often than any other prophet in the Qur'an as Musa.
figures.facet: The corpus held him only in the Samaritan minority reading (samaritanism-moses, 'the Sole Prophet') — the root Exodus lawgiver that reading refracts, and that all three mainstream faiths independently claim as their own, was itself absent until now.
figures.provenance: Exodus–Deuteronomy (Masoretic Text); Qur'an 2:51–61, 20:9–98 (Musa); Hebrews 3 and Acts 7 for the New Testament's typological reading.

date.note: If historical, conventionally placed in the 13th century BCE under Ramesside Egypt; no extrabiblical record of Moses or the Exodus has been found, so this is a traditional setting, not attested history.

figures.gloss: The first man and woman of Genesis 2–3, formed from earth and rib in the garden of Eden, whose eating of the forbidden fruit brings expulsion, labor, and mortality into the world. Islam reads their eating as a forgivable lapse rather than an inherited stain, and installs Adam as the first prophet and vice-regent (khalifa) on earth.
figures.facet: The tradition's whole account of the human condition hangs on this one couple, yet the corpus held only their esoteric abstraction, Adam Kadmon, the mystics' cosmic template — never the ordinary first parents whose disobedience the mystical Adam exists to correct.
figures.provenance: Genesis 2–3 (Masoretic Text); Qur'an 2:30–39, 7:19–25 (Adam and Hawwa); Elaine Pagels, Adam, Eve, and the Serpent (1988).

figures.gloss: The righteous man of Genesis 6–9 who builds the ark at God's command, survives the flood that destroys a corrupted world with his family and paired animals, and receives the rainbow covenant that God will never again destroy the earth by water. The Qur'an names him Nuh, a prophet sent to warn a disbelieving people who reject him.
figures.facet: The Near East's great deluge, told in Mesopotamian myth (Utnapishtim, Ziusudra) centuries before Genesis was written down, gives the Abrahamic composite one of its clearest possible gold seams — yet no flood figure existed anywhere in the corpus index until now.
figures.provenance: Genesis 6–9 (Masoretic Text); Qur'an 11:25–48, 71 (Nuh); Alexander Heidel, The Gilgamesh Epic and Old Testament Parallels (1946), on the shared Mesopotamian flood template.

figures.gloss: The messenger-archangel who interprets Daniel's visions of the end times, announces to Mary in Luke's Gospel that she will bear Jesus, and — as Jibril — is said to have dictated the Qur'an to Muhammad over twenty-three years, beginning in the cave at Hira. The one angel named and active across all three scriptures.
figures.facet: A single messenger threading three revelations that otherwise claim to supersede one another — Daniel's interpreter, the Annunciation's herald, and the Qur'an's conduit are, on each tradition's own telling, the same being doing the same job three times.

figures.gloss: The many-headed sea-dragon of old Hebrew poetry — the primeval chaos-beast whose heads YHWH crushes in Psalm 74:13–14 and whose terrible strength is catalogued at length in Job 41. Isaiah 27:1 promises its final slaying at the end of days; later rabbinic tradition sets its flesh as the banquet of the righteous in the world to come.
figures.facet: The crushed monster behind YHWH's own storm-god inheritance — the chaos this tradition's God overcomes to become sovereign is itself unrostered until now, though the yahweh shard's own gloss turns on exactly this defeat.
figures.provenance: Psalm 74:13–14, Isaiah 27:1, Job 41 (Masoretic Text); Mark S. Smith, The Early History of God (2002), on YHWH's Chaoskampf inheritance from the Canaanite Yam/Litan cycle.

figures.gloss: The son of David who builds the First Temple in Jerusalem, whose proverbial wisdom is tested by the Queen of Sheba, and whose reign later tradition remembers as the high-water mark of Israelite kingship. The Qur'an names him Sulayman, a prophet-king given dominion over the jinn, the wind, and the speech of birds.
figures.facet: In-corpus he survived only as the grimoire tradition's demon-binder (goetia-solomon) and a Masonic temple-symbol — the wise, temple-building king those later magical and fraternal elaborations both grew from was itself the missing root.
figures.provenance: 1 Kings 3–11 (Masoretic Text); Qur'an 27:15–44, 34:12–14 (Sulayman); the pseudepigraphal Testament of Solomon (1st–3rd c. CE), source of the later magical Solomon.

date.note: Conventional biblical chronology for Solomon's reign; the scale and even historicity of a unified Solomonic kingdom are debated in the archaeological literature (the 'United Monarchy' controversy).

edges.note: Early Israelite religion adapts Canaanite storm-and-Chaoskampf imagery: Baʿal/Yam → YHWH/Leviathan.
The dragon-slaying storm-god who becomes the supreme ordering God of his city, then his world.
Death, burial and rising to rule — but Frazer over-read this family; Christianity insists on history where Egypt meant renewal. Held at arm's length, honestly.
Tree at the center, four directions / four rivers running to the quarters of the world.
The unconditioned ground from which a knowable, named divinity unfolds — a far reach across very different grammars.
Both supreme, unmade, beyond image — but the parallel is interpretive; Akan Nyame recedes where YHWH demands covenant. Flagged as a stretch.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the infinite, unmanifest, and ultimately incomprehensible Godhead from which all other divine attributes emanate.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Alawite Quddas actively incorporated elements of the Christian Eucharist, including the sacramental use of wine, during centuries of shared Levantine geography.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The supreme reality that remains entirely ungraspable except through its emanated manifestations and the esoteric gates.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A direct esoteric reconceptualization of the biblical Jesus Christ into a macrocosmic, ongoing Solar entity.

The defining historical adjacency of the mirror: 'Allah' (al-ilah, 'the God') is the same Semitic high-god word as Hebrew 'El/Eloah', and the Meccan supreme creator was reidentified as the one God of Abraham — pagan high-god to monotheist absolute by documented continuity.

The Arabian betyl-cult of sacred standing stones is cousin to the Canaanite/Israelite massebah and the stone of Bethel ('house of God') Jacob anointed — a shared Semitic holy-stone practice, the same family of aniconic centre.
Both are the sacred centre of the world, the navel where heaven meets earth — the Kaaba as omphalos-shrine rhyming with the quartered garden at the cosmos's heart.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The esoteric literalization of Exodus 3:14, where 'I AM' is reinterpreted from God's distant name to the practitioner's own higher self.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly framed in the teachings as the successor to Jesus's planetary office, stepping in to sponsor the Aquarian Age just as Christ sponsored the Piscean Age.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both function as incarnate liberators whose divine missions are explicitly tied to the elevation of the marginalized and the upending of earthly hierarchies.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Historically, Christian and Islamic missionaries directly mapped Katonda onto the Abrahamic God to facilitate conversion.
GOLD SEAM: Both denote the ultimate, infinite, and entirely unknowable Reality that precedes all emanation and definition.
GOLD SEAM: Bahá'u'lláh explicitly claims to be the 'Return of Christ in the glory of the Father', an intentional theological bridging of Christian eschatology.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The unmanifest, infinite creator who speaks reality into existence through alphabetic or linguistic vibration.
Both punish the lie, the false oath and the proud — moral order enforced by the supreme power; offered only as a structural echo across a vast gulf.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both identify as the God of Abraham, but BHI theology explicitly confines his covenant to a specific genetic lineage, rejecting Christian universalism.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Syncretic identification, but entirely recontextualized as a militaristic, Black liberator rather than the passive, universal savior of Western orthodoxy.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The concept of the Infinite Creator without end, strictly devoid of physical attributes or incarnations.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Ram Mohan Roy explicitly extracted the moral teachings of Jesus in 'The Precepts of Jesus', decoupling them from miracles to construct a universal rationalist ethic.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both function as cosmic saviors who voluntarily enter the realm of suffering and sacrifice their own immediate bliss to secure the salvation of all beings.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both traditions conceptualize the ultimate absolute as a paradoxical 'nothingness' (Ayin/Sunyata) from which all relative reality endlessly emanates.

Speculative: the Bodhi-tree temptation rhymes with the wilderness temptation of Christ by Satan — the tempter who tests the awakener with power and doubt. A structural rhyme of motif, no contact; flagged.
The temptation-and-victory pattern: a saviour assailed by the prince of this world and overcoming through refusal — Bodhi tree and wilderness as the same narrative joint, independently arrived at.
The formless absolute (dharmakāya / Ein Sof) that emanates the manifest worlds — a structural parallel of the unmanifest ground, no contact.
GOLD SEAM: Through intense historical syncretism, the Yoruba creator of bodies became physically identified with Jesus ('Senhor do Bonfim') in Bahia.
GOLD SEAM: The transcendent Yoruba creator mapped directly onto the Abrahamic God under the intense historical pressure of Brazilian Catholicism.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Cao Đài doctrine explicitly names Jehovah as an earlier, localized manifestation of the Supreme Being.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Jesus Christ is explicitly incorporated into Caodaist history as the central redeeming figure of the Second Universal Amnesty.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: John Frum's prophecies heavily borrow from, and subvert, Christian missionary teachings of the Second Coming and apocalypse.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Helen Schucman explicitly bridged her channeled text to traditional Christianity by identifying the voice as the historical Jesus Christ.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The multidimensional entity mirrors the Kabbalistic Infinite, projecting localized fragments of itself into the lower physical spheres to experience manifestation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both describe the infinite, unknowable zero-point of existence that cannot be directly apprehended by the conscious mind.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Cheondoism formed explicitly in opposition to Western Catholicism, rejecting a distant, judging Creator in favor of an intimately internal divine.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A supreme deity incarnated in a specific historical moment as a human to perform a cosmic intervention that permanently alters reality.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Historically, Christian missionaries in Malawi directly mapped Chiuta onto the Abrahamic God in Bible translations.
GOLD SEAM: Literal same-god identity; the specific Christian elaboration of the shared Abrahamic Messiah.
GOLD SEAM: Early Christian theology radically identified Jesus directly with Yahweh, applying Old Testament texts about the God of Israel to the crucified carpenter.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both feature a central sacrament of drinking a shared fluid to forge a profound, unbreakable bond of spiritual kinship.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The literal or mystical washing in the blood of a blameless victim to secure eternal spiritual rebirth and wash away the old self.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Clockmaker is historically derived directly from Yahweh, but systematically stripped of miracles, wrath, covenants, and intervention.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A formless, elevated creator who dictates absolute moral order and breathes the animating spirit of life into existence.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The deliberate substitution of a blameless, sacred victim to fully absorb the spiritual debt and death meant for humanity.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Pythagorean numerology historically syncretized with Kabbalistic emanation, mapping the ten points of the tetractys directly to the ten Sephirot emanating from the infinite.
A perfect being voluntarily sacrificed and resurrected to purify and redeem a disordered creation; a structural rhyme, flagged, with no contact implied.
The infinite undifferentiated source contracting and unfolding into the categories of creation — world-egg as a rhyme with Ein Sof's emanation, flagged.
Both seed humankind from a counted, ordered origin laid out in directions and grains/rivers — but the Dogon count eight paired ancestors against a single first couple; a flagged structural rhyme.
Both lay the cosmos out as a quartered, direction-bearing plan radiating from a central order; offered cautiously as a cosmogram-rhyme.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent the entirely unknowable, infinite Divine Reality that can only be approached or understood through its distinct emanations.
GOLD SEAM: Both function as the temporal, physical locus of the Divine who departs the earthly plane with the promise of an eschatological return.
GOLD SEAM: Historically, the Midianite Jethro (Shu'ayb) is deeply entwined with the earliest transmission of monotheism to Moses, framing him as a primal conduit of the one God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both traditions posit an infinite, unmanifest absolute that can only be approached through its descending emanations.
GOLD SEAM: Divine figures who descend into the realm of the dead and return, offering a formalized path of personal soteriology and eternal life.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Santo Daime doctrine explicitly identifies Juramidam as the return of Jesus Christ, incarnated in the Amazonian brew.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: In Fang Bwiti, the creator god Nzam/Bwiti is explicitly syncretized with the Abrahamic God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are foundational, enclosed gardens representing an ideal state of human innocence and peace, functioning as sanctuaries from the fallen world.
Both project a quartered, centred cosmos as sacred map — the templum divided into orienting regions as Eden's garden parts into four rivers from the centre.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: In 19th-century colonial syncretism (such as the Tuka movement), indigenous prophets explicitly identified the Creator serpent Degei with the biblical Jehovah.

The Kalevala closes with Väinämöinen departing by sea before a virgin-born child, vowing to return when needed — a parting shaped by the Christian Christ overlaid on an older returning-savior motif; a syncretic overprint, not a true cognate.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the unmanifest, boundless, and incomprehensible source from which reality emanates downward in successively heavier structural stages.
GOLD SEAM: Esoteric Freemasonry, especially in the Royal Arch and Scottish Rite, explicitly syncretized the Lost Word with Kabbalistic concepts of the Tetragrammaton and the infinite.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Wovoka explicitly identified himself, and was identified by Plains followers, as the returned Christ.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Direct incorporation of the Protestant Christian God into the Paiute vision of the supreme creator.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicit theological appropriation of the Christian savior, conceptually weaponized against his European worshippers.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The longing for a return to an uncorrupted, pre-fall ecological paradise brimming with spontaneous abundance.

The scandal at the tradition's core: the gnostics read the Creator of Genesis ('I am God and there is no other') AS Yaldabaoth — the jealous demiurge mistaking himself for the One. A documented inversion of the Abrahamic creator.

Mani held Jesus among the true apostles of Light and called himself an apostle of Jesus Christ; Manichaean texts venerate a Jesus of Light. A deliberate, documented incorporation of Christ into the fused religion.

Hermetic ascent through emanated spheres toward the unknowable One closely parallels the Kabbalist climb through the sefirot to Ein Sof — and the two streams actually cross-fertilised in the Renaissance.

The unknowable Source beyond being from which graded emanations flow is shared structure; Lurianic Kabbalah's contraction-and-fall of the divine echoes the gnostic Pleroma, via documented late-antique and medieval transmission.
Sophia's exile and yearning return is the attested prototype of Kabbalah's Shekhinah, the divine presence fallen into exile with the world — fallen-feminine-wisdom transmitted into Jewish mysticism.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Goetic operator literally speaks as Yahweh, assuming His authority and names to command the demons.
GOLD SEAM: The GD entirely adopted the Jewish Kabbalistic framework of emanations descending from the infinite Ein Sof, though heavily repurposing it for operative magic.
GOLD SEAM: The Order explicitly mapped the paths and Sephiroth onto the fall of man and the four rivers of Eden in their initiation texts.

A holy tree at the world's centre sending its roots or rivers to the four quarters — the Tree of Peace at Onondaga against Eden's tree and four rivers; the axis-tree reaching to the four directions, imagined twice.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Directly borrowed and adapted from Jewish mysticism, though Hermeticism strips it of its strict monotheistic boundaries.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A Renaissance Christian Kabbalistic adaptation of the Tetragrammaton, intended to embed Christ into the ultimate Jewish name of God.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Hoodoo appropriates the scriptural texts of Yahweh, forcibly stripping them of orthodox Christian theology and wielding them as mechanical incantations for earthly dominance.

A reach: both supreme, unmade, source of all — but Chukwu RECEDES from cult where YHWH demands covenant, and the Igbo equation of Chukwu with the Christian God is itself a contested colonial-era identification. Flagged as interpretive.
GOLD SEAM: The explicit, documented theological identification of the God of the Qur'an with the God of Abraham and Moses.
GOLD SEAM: Structurally, the Qur'an occupies the exact theological space in Islam that Christ does in Christianity: the eternal, uncreated Word of God entering the physical world.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent the absolute, indivisible source of reality that entirely transcends human categorization and physical incarnation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Revival Zion actively syncretizes Christian apostles and angelic hosts into distinct, summonable bands that operate through African-derived possession.

GOLD SEAM: Both are anointed figures who bring redemption — but the Jewish Messiah is a human king who restores the Davidic monarchy, while Christ is the divine Son of God who dies for sins. The two are radically different in nature and role, though historically related.
GOLD SEAM: The Kabbalistic conceptualization of the exact same ultimate reality present in the broader Abrahamic tradition.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: During colonial missionization, Ngai was explicitly adopted as the translation for the Abrahamic God, leading to profound historical and theological syncretism.
Both unmade, supreme, beyond image — but interpretive only: Nzambi recedes from cult where YHWH demands covenant. Flagged as a reach.
A cross / quartering at the centre of the world dividing the cosmos into four — the BaKongo sun's four moments against Eden's four rivers from one source. Quartered-cosmos parallel, no contact.
Both a single supreme heaven-lord who decrees and recedes from the lived world; a speculative cross-family reach, flagged.
The quartered cosmos centred on a holy axis — the sacred hoop crossed by the four winds against Eden's tree fed by four rivers to the four quarters; the cross-in-circle world-map, twice imagined.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Luciferianism explicitly and historically constructs its Demiurge as the direct inversion of the Abrahamic Yahweh, viewing him as an ignorant tyrant rather than a loving father.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A structural and moral inversion; Christ offers salvation through submission and vicarious atonement, while Lucifer demands self-liberation and absolute intellectual sovereignty.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A supreme, non-iconic deity of the heights who delivers both covenantal favor and terrible, fiery wrath.
GOLD SEAM: The boundless, infinite source of light that emanates lesser divine beings but remains incomprehensible in itself.
GOLD SEAM: Mandaean texts explicitly polemicize against Jesus, depicting him as a false messiah who subverted the true, saving baptismal teachings of Manda d-Hayyi.
GOLD SEAM: Historically, Mandaeism consciously demotes the orthodox Abrahamic creator to the status of a bungling, lower-tier demiurge, explicitly reversing Genesis.
GOLD SEAM: The concept of sacred, living waters originating from a divine, supernal realm that sustain life and spiritual purity on earth.

GOLD SEAM: Both are ritual meals of bread and wine that commemorate a sacrificial death — the Mithraic meal reenacts the feast of Mithras and Sol, the Christian Eucharist reenacts the Last Supper. The parallel was noted by early Christian apologists (Justin Martyr, Tertullian).
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Yogananda explicitly taught that Mahavatar Babaji and Jesus Christ work together in continuous communion to send the science of Kriya Yoga to save the West.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are envisioned as exclusively supreme, hyper-personal creators who demand absolute devotion and operate totally distinct from the pantheons of other gods.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The boundless, ineffable void that emits the initial rays of light to spark the cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Elijah Muhammad explicitly identified W.D. Fard as the incarnate fulfillment of the biblical Jehovah, the Long-Awaited Messiah.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Fard is theologically identified as the returning Son of Man from the New Testament, collapsing Christian eschatology into his physical arrival in Detroit.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Mother Plane is a mechanized reinterpretation of Ezekiel's biblical vision, transforming the divine chariot of God into a literal, apocalyptic spacecraft.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicit, documented theological merging of the indigenous supreme creator with the Abrahamic God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Direct adoption of Jesus of Nazareth, reinterpreted structurally to serve as a marginalized, indigenous culture hero.
A sacred centre fixed by a fourfold scheme — four mountains binding Dinétah, four rivers parting from Eden; the quartered axis-mundi independently witnessed.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Historical transmission—Neoplatonic emanationism profoundly shaped medieval Kabbalistic conceptions of the infinite, hidden, and attributeless God.
GOLD SEAM: New Thought directly co-opts the biblical creator God, stripping him of personality, wrath, and judgment to reforge him as a neutral, benevolent principle.
GOLD SEAM: Syncretically redefines the historical Jesus of Nazareth, separating the man from the 'Christ'—which is reinterpreted as a universal state of divine consciousness.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the infinite, unmanifested source of reality that is too vast to be conceptualized or directly interacted with by humanity.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Through his fusion with John the Baptist, Shango structurally mirrors the catalytic, baptizing initiation of the Christian narrative, albeit transformed by thunder.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Spinoza explicitly redefined the Abrahamic Yahweh into a mechanistic, non-personal universe, triggering his excommunication.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both signify the infinite, unknowable, and limitless foundation from which all finite attributes emanate.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The FSM explicitly mimics the Old Testament God's jealousy, creation mechanics, and arbitrary commandments (the 'I'd Really Rather You Didn'ts').
GOLD SEAM: Ancient, bearded Levantine patriarchs who rule through covenants, demanding absolute obedience and fire-offerings from their chosen people.
GOLD SEAM: Directly entangled in biblical history; the Punic 'mlk' (votive offering) was demonized by Israelite prophets into the monstrous idol 'Moloch', marking a violent theological schism.
GOLD SEAM: Jah is a direct linguistic and theological inheritance of the Biblical Yahweh, re-contextualized into a living Black messiah.
GOLD SEAM: Haile Selassie is explicitly worshipped as the returned Christ in his Kingly character, fulfilling biblical prophecy.
GOLD SEAM: The primordial, uncorrupted paradise of human origin, envisioned not just as a mythic past but a geographic destiny.
GOLD SEAM: C.R.C. is modeled as a deliberate esoteric 'imitatio Christi', offering a path to salvation through occult science rather than dogmatic faith.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The identical God of Israel, derived from the same ancient root, though Samaritanism preserves a strictly non-emanational theology compared to later Rabbinic Judaism.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the ultimate unknowable God, though Samaritanism fiercely rejects the Jewish Kabbalistic idea that God generates subsequent sephirotic emanations.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Samaritan theology explicitly identifies Mount Gerizim as the unflooded summit of the Garden of Eden from the shared Genesis account.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are eschatological figures tied to salvation, though the Taheb is a purely human restorer of the Law, explicitly lacking divine sonship.
Two answers to why humankind lost immortality — Eden makes death a punishment for transgression, the moon-and-hare a delivery failure and a mourner's disbelief. Same question, opposite moral logic.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: TST's literary Satan is fundamentally constructed as the necessary, empathetic opposition to the tyrannical authority of the Abrahamic God.
GOLD SEAM: LaVey specifically codified his symbolic Satan as the direct, earthly inversion of Christ's spiritual pacifism and self-denial.
GOLD SEAM: Satanism actively repudiates the Abrahamic God, replacing subservience to an external creator with absolute self-sovereignty.
GOLD SEAM: Where the Kabbalistic infinite is unknowable and boundless, the Satanic god is localized entirely within the finite, carnal human ego.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The infinite, boundless reality that precedes and encompasses all things, possessing no human-like personality.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: In Catholic Serer liturgy, the name Roog Sene is officially used as the direct translation for the Abrahamic God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The infinite, boundless, unmanifest absolute that precedes and contains all cosmic emanation without being diminished by it.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly fused by Zimbabwean independent churches, identifying the cave-speaking Musikavanhu with the biblical God.
GOLD SEAM: The infinite, formless, and genderless source of all reality that emanates into the universe without compromising its indivisible unity.
GOLD SEAM: Constantine fused Sol Invictus and Christ early in his reign, utilizing solar iconography for Jesus as the 'Sun of Righteousness' to ease the empire's transition to monotheism.

GOLD SEAM (disputed): The shared December 25th date is commonly argued to be the early Church adopting the Dies Natalis Solis Invicti — but this 'History of Religions' borrowing is contested (the rival Calculation hypothesis dates the nativity independently of any solar feast). Held at tier 3 pending the corpus audit.
GOLD SEAM: Spiritualism's Infinite Intelligence shares the unbounded, non-anthropomorphic qualities of the Kabbalistic Infinite.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Johannine Christianity explicitly adopted the Hellenistic/Stoic concept of the 'Logos' (the Word) and incarnated it into a historical person.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The Apostle Paul at the Areopagus quoted the Stoic poet Aratus ('For we are indeed his offspring') to identify the Stoic immanent Zeus with the Abrahamic God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Aradia actively mirrors Christ's mission but substitutes meekness and forgiveness with vengeance, poison, and peasant rebellion.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The cursed biblical wanderer is elevated by the witches into a figure of sympathy, entirely rejecting Yahweh's original punitive judgment.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Swedenborg explicitly identifies his Divine Human with the historical Jesus Christ, reinterpreted not as a separate person of the Trinity but as the sole, integrated God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Swedenborg directly equates Jehovah of the Old Testament with the inner soul of Jesus, asserting absolute identity rather than relational separation.
GOLD SEAM: The incarnation of the ultimate cosmic principle (the Dao / the Logos) descending into human history to provide revelation and salvation.
Both a single supreme sky-power who legitimates rulership and recedes from images; a speculative reach across families, flagged as such.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A singular, supreme creator deity who intervenes directly in human history and demands ultimate devotion.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The exact, localized geographical origin point of humanity that holds the key to mankind's ultimate spiritual restoration.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicit theological reversal; Satan is worshipped as the liberating hero, while Yahweh is condemned as the tyrannical cosmic enslaver.
GOLD SEAM: The Mass of the Phoenix is a conscious, systematic inversion of the Christian Eucharist, redirecting the sacrifice from Jesus to the practitioner's own ego.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent the ultimate, unknowable, and limitless divine reality that precedes all cosmic emanation.
GOLD SEAM: Soteriological figures whose physical descent into the earth and subsequent return forms the foundational proof of eternal life for their followers.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both describe an infinite, unmanifest absolute from which all manifest reality emanates.

FLAGGED: the missionary scar made explicit — Jesuit catechists from Anchieta on identified the thunder Tupã with the one God of scripture, and 'Tupã' became the standard Guaraní word for the Christian God; a documented elevation of a storm-power to monotheist creator.

FLAGGED: both name an earthly paradise of deathlessness and effortless plenty — but Eden is the garden humankind was expelled FROM, the Land-Without-Evil the garden the Guaraní walk TOWARD; the lost paradise behind versus the sought paradise ahead. (The very systematisation of the Guaraní myth may carry Christian-eschatological colouring — Shapiro 1987.)
GOLD SEAM: Raëlism explicitly reinterprets the biblical Yahweh as a plural term for the extraterrestrial scientists, stripping the deity of supernaturalism.
GOLD SEAM: The Ashtar Command mythology explicitly incorporates Jesus as 'Sananda', the supreme spiritual commander of the etheric fleet.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly and formally identified with Jesus Christ in Umbanda, reinterpreting the Yoruba deity as a Kardecist avatar of moral perfection.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The ultimate, infinite, and inherently unknowable divine source from which all differentiated spiritual forces emanate.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The literal, scriptural root of Universalism, originally argued by founders as the inescapable conclusion of Christ's total victory over death.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: While Eden's rivers flow outward from a single divine source to the world, the Water Communion draws scattered worldly waters inward to construct the sacred center.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The boundless, un-differentiated divine reality that precedes, animates, and ultimately saturates all individual religious expression.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly identifies the historical figure of Jesus with the universe-creator Michael, drastically expanding his cosmic resume.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The text explicitly attempts to course-correct the Abrahamic conception of Yahweh, stripping away tribal wrath to reveal the Universal Father.
GOLD SEAM: Regional patron deities elevated to supreme, jealous national gods whose cults centralize state identity and military survival.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The profound structural theological commitment that the absolute must take on finite, suffering flesh to save a broken creation.
GOLD SEAM: The Catholic God absorbed completely into the Vodou cosmology as the ultimate, albeit withdrawn, creator.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The infinite, unmanifest Godhead that cannot be known in itself, only through its successive emanations.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The direct bodily incarnation of the Divine Word, descending to act as the ultimate bridge between humanity and the absolute.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent an entirely unknowable, infinite divine reality that only interacts with creation through a series of structured emanations.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Jesus is literally incorporated into the Yiguandao pantheon as one of the Five Messengers representing the Red Sun era.
Resurrection of the dead, final judgement and a world made new — Zoroastrian eschatology is widely held to have shaped Second-Temple and Christian apocalyptic during Persian rule over Judea.
The virgin-born future redeemer who raises the dead at the end of days — a documented likely influence on the messianic template, via the Achaemenid/Parthian contact with Judaism.

The principled Adversary as a cosmic figure (ha-satan grown into a true Devil) enters Abrahamic thought during and after the Babylonian exile under Persian dominion — historical contact, contested degree.
Yima's vara built to outlast a world-ending winter rhymes with Noah's ark before the flood — a shared flood-survival/ark motif, no descent claimed.
The One refracted into emanated aspects/virtues — a structural rhyme with the sefirot flowing from Ein Sof, the unknowable refracting into nameable powers.
Two near-monotheist creator-lords of Truth in historical contact under the Achaemenids; the structural and possibly genetic kinship of ethical monotheism.
Two answers to why humankind lost immortality — Eden makes death a punishment for transgression, the chameleon makes it a delivery failure. Same question, opposite moral logic.
The text's own authorship claim: the dictating Voice is identified as Jesus and speaks in the first person about his crucifixion — attested as the tradition's self-understanding, not as history.
GOLD SEAM: Explicit doctrinal identification of the Roza Bal saint Yuz Asaf with Jesus of Nazareth — attested Ahmadi teaching since 1899, rejected by historians.
The Repairer is explicitly Christ, re-read through the reintegration doctrine as the reopener of humanity's lost divine function.

Gap fix: literal same-god identity left unbound in-corpus — the composite's YHWH and rabbinic Judaism's Tetragrammaton-God are the identical deity, named once for the shared root and once for its developed tradition.
Direct covenant recipient: the unilateral covenant of Genesis 15 and 17 is cut with Abraham by name.
The Sinai theophany and the giving of the Law is the central meeting between YHWH and Moses.
The rainbow covenant of Genesis 9 is cut between YHWH and Noah — the Hebrew Bible's first covenant.
The chaos-monster the yahweh shard's own gloss describes crushing; Psalm 74 and Job 41 supply the beast this entry restores.
Adam and Eve are placed by God within the garden this figure describes; the tree of life and four rivers furnish their home before the Fall.
Gabriel's Annunciation to Mary in Luke 1 is the direct narrative hinge introducing Christ's incarnation.
Literal same-prophet identity: Samaritanism's 'Sole Prophet' is this same Exodus lawgiver, elevated in Samaritan doctrine above all subsequent revelation and prophecy.
As Jibril, this same messenger is credited in Islamic doctrine with delivering the Qur'an to Muhammad across twenty-three years of revelation beginning at Hira.
The Ars Goetia's demon-binding magician is built directly on the biblical temple-building king, his name and legend borrowed wholesale for the grimoire's frame narrative.
Freemasonic ritual is staged around the building of this same king's Temple, its floor-plan and craftsman Hiram Abiff forming the Order's central symbolic architecture.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: the orthodox Jesus reread as an ascended human who earned rather than uniquely possessed his exaltation — consistent with the file's existing Saint-Germain/Christ seam.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Sen's New Dispensation explicitly incorporated Christ as a fulfillment-figure within a claimed universal synthesis of faiths, delivering addresses like 'That Marvellous Mystery — The Trinity' that reinterpret Christian doctrine inside a Brahmo frame.
Dagon's idol falls prostrate and is decapitated before the Ark of YHWH in the Philistine temple at Ashdod (1 Samuel 5) — a direct textual staging of one god's cult humbled by another's.

Speculative and contested: Freud's Moses and Monotheism proposed a historical descent from Atenism to Israelite monotheism; mainstream Egyptology and biblical scholarship do not support direct transmission. Logged as a reach, not an identification.

Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: the gnostic Savior is explicitly docetic, wearing flesh without truly suffering it — the very position 1 John 4:2 and Ignatius of Antioch name and condemn as heresy against the mainstream Christ.
Mandaean narrative attributes Jesus's Jerusalem miracles to Anush Uthra, who exposes him as a false messiah — an inversion parallel to the existing Manda d-Hayyi/Christ polemic.

A savior figure who descends through the realms of the dead before the world's founding, binding its powers and seizing its secrets — Hibil Ziwa's harrowing of the worlds of darkness against the Christian Harrowing of Hell.
Constantine's solar coinage and 321 Sunday law carried the cult's monotheistic sun-sovereign structure directly into his patronage of Christianity.

Christian doctrine identifies the Trinity's first person directly with the covenant God of Israel while adding two co-equal persons — the same God of Abraham reorganized into a triune structure Judaism does not recognize.

Ihoa o ngā Mano is the same YHWH the abrahamic shard holds as Yahweh, addressed in Rātana liturgy through an extended court invoked directly after the Trinity — the Faithful Angels and the Māngai as Mouthpiece — the doctrinal extension over which the mission churches broke with the movement in the mid-1920s.

In Christianity the Word incarnate is crucified once at Golgotha and rises bodily; in Manichaeism the same name denotes light dispersed and continuously crucified in all vegetal matter, freed piecemeal by the Elect's ascetic meal rather than by a single historical resurrection — one name, incompatible christologies.

Textual fact, not analogy: roughly half the Circle Seven Koran (1927) reproduces Levi Dowling's Aquarian Gospel of Jesus the Christ (1908) verbatim, recasting the same Jesus as an Aquarian adept perfecting Christ-consciousness through travel and initiation, against the Word-made-flesh of the source tradition.

At the umbrella-Abrahamic grain, Judaism, Christianity, and Islam collectively hold Moses among a continuing prophetic line; Samaritanism narrows the entire scripture and prophetic office to him alone — same historical figure, opposite exclusivity claim.

dossier.essence: The family of monotheisms — Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, with ancient Israelite religion as the root — that trace themselves to the God of Abraham. Together its branches claim over half the world's population; this record treats them as a composite shard, one covenantal lineage refracted into rival canons.
dossier.cosmos: One transcendent God creates the world by will or word, stands outside it, and acts within history — time runs in a line from creation toward judgment, not in cycles. Early Israelite religion knew only a shadowy Sheol; bodily resurrection and last judgment developed in the Second Temple period and became central to Christianity and Islam, while rabbinic Judaism holds resurrection alongside the world-to-come. Prophets and revealed scripture bridge the gulf between creator and creature.
dossier.practice: Each branch institutionalizes covenant differently: Torah observance, synagogue, and halakha in Judaism; sacraments, church, and creed in Christianity; the Five Pillars, mosque, and sharia in Islam. Common to all are congregational prayer, scriptural reading, fasting and festival calendars, almsgiving, and ethical demands understood as owed to God rather than negotiated with him.
dossier.canon: The Hebrew Bible (Tanakh); the Christian Bible adding the New Testament; the Qur'an — each surrounded by an authoritative interpretive corpus (Talmud, patristic writings, hadith).
dossier.reflects: The covenantal impulse: one exclusive God whose will is disclosed in scripture and whose arena is linear history — religion as binding contract between creator and community.
sources: Mark S. Smith, 'The Early History of God' (1990)
F. E. Peters, 'The Children of Abraham' (1982)
